chief_complaint: A 45-year-old male patient presented with chest pain and shortness of breath.
history: The patient has a history of hypertension and hyperlipidemia. He has been experiencing chest pain and shortness of breath for the past 2 hours. He has no history of smoking or previous cardiac events.
exam: Vital signs: BP 160/90 mmHg, HR 120 bpm, RR 24/min. Physical examination revealed a tender and displaced apex beat.
labs: ECG: ST-segment elevation in leads II, III, and aVF. Bloods: Troponin 10 ng/mL, CK-MB 50 ng/mL. Imaging: Echocardiogram showing a large anterior wall myocardial infarction.
correct_diagnosis: Acute Myocardial Infarction due to Coronary Artery Disease
differentials: Acute Myocardial Infarction due to Coronary Artery Disease
Acute Myocardial Infarction due to Cardiac Amyloidosis
Acute Myocardial Infarction due to Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy
Acute Myocardial Infarction due to Takotsubo Cardiomyopathy
Acute Myocardial Infarction due to Pulmonary Embolism
correct_ranking: Acute Myocardial Infarction due to Coronary Artery Disease
Acute Myocardial Infarction due to Cardiac Amyloidosis
Acute Myocardial Infarction due to Hypertrophic Cardiomyopathy
Acute Myocardial Infarction due to Takotsubo Cardiomyopathy
Acute Myocardial Infarction due to Pulmonary Embolism
